finding: Multi-needle retrieval is only measurable on Qwen2-7B (strong instruction following). Pythia-6.9B (base model, no instruction tuning) cannot perform multi-needle QA even at FP16 baseline (0/21). Mistral-7B shows inconsistent baseline performance (7/26), making quantization impact unmeasurable. On Qwen2-7B, the pattern is perfectly clear: naive4 0/26, nsep+pchan4 26/26.
limitation: Multi-needle QA requires instruction-following capability. Base models (Pythia) cannot be evaluated with this protocol. Single-needle with simple prompt format (previous experiments) is more universally applicable.
